vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential salt-fog corrosion on aluminum components.
risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coating to aluminum parts.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for moving parts in GPS boom mount.
risk.mit: Increase clearance to 0.5mm for smooth rotation.

risk.desc: Limited access for maintenance in avionics bay.
risk.mit: Design larger access panels for easier maintenance.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may affect GPS boom alignment.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion joints in GPS boom mount.

risk.desc: Camera window may fog in humid conditions.
risk.mit: Install anti-fog coating on camera window.

risk.desc: Design assumes maritime surveillance without specific corrosion protection.
risk.mit: Implement salt-fog resistant coatings and materials.

risk.desc: Landing gear flotation not detailed in the design.
risk.mit: Specify and design flotation-equipped landing gear.

risk.desc: No specific verification plan for high-stress areas.
risk.mit: Develop a detailed verification plan for high-stress areas.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal stress due to wide temperature range.
risk.mit: Include thermal stress analysis and mitigation in design.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to insufficient mass budget.
risk.mit: Review and adjust mass budget to ensure structural integrity.

verify.rule: Ensure all required fields are present in the blueprint.
Ensure the estimated mass is within the design constraints.
Ensure all P0 internal_feature_targets are present in the geometry_ops.
Ensure all interfaces have reciprocal definitions in adjacent subsystems.
Ensure critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.
Ensure boundary conditions are defined for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.desc: Material selection may not be optimal for the maritime environment, consider corrosion-resistant materials.
risk.mit: Conduct material compatibility tests and select appropriate materials for salt-fog resistance.

risk.desc: The design may not fully account for the thermal expansion and contraction in the maritime environment.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion joints and conduct thermal analysis to ensure proper fit and function.

risk.desc: The cooling fins may not provide sufficient heat dissipation under high load conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal simulations and redesign the cooling fins if necessary.

risk.desc: The harness access panel may not be easily accessible for maintenance in the field.
risk.mit: Design the harness access panel to be easily removable and conduct field tests for accessibility.

risk.desc: The fastener pattern may not provide sufficient preload for critical joints.
risk.mit: Conduct preload analysis and adjust the fastener pattern if necessary.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP and aluminum components.
risk.mit: Use expansion-compensating materials or design with thermal expansion gaps.

risk.desc: Risk of harness strain relief failure due to repeated flexing at the tilt axis.
risk.mit: Implement more robust strain relief geometry and review bend radius requirements.

risk.desc: Service hatch may not be fully accessible in tight spaces.
risk.mit: Ensure service hatch design allows for full access in all operational positions.

risk.desc: Incorrect connector keying could lead to mis-mating and system failure.
risk.mit: Double-check connector keying and labeling to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Potential for fastener loosening due to vibration and environmental stress.
risk.mit: Use vibration-resistant fasteners and periodic maintenance checks.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

physics_paths.path_description: Tilt-axis control harness runs from harness_channel through tilt_axis to nacelle control systems.
Thermal management via service_hatch, allowing for heat dissipation.
Tilt-axis rotation allows nacelle to tilt from forward flight to vertical lift position.
Service window provides access for maintenance and inspection of tilt-axis components.
Load path from nacelle to wing structure via nacelle_mount.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: align tilt_axis with nacelle_mount before installing revolute_bearing
align nacelle_mount with wing_structure before installing bolts
route harness through harness_channel before connecting to tilt_axis

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and CMM
manufacturing_strategy.implications: FDM for complex geometry, CNC for precise interfaces
